CannonadeCommand – firepower and finish for Unraid's Docker, Plugins and VM tabs

Dependency-aware start orchestration, live resource limits with proof, and clean themeable badges — injected into the native pages.

   

What is this?

An Unraid plugin that gives the native Docker, Plugins and VM tabs both muscle and a makeover — without replacing a single page. On the Docker tab it swaps start-order guesswork for a real start plan: containers declare what they depend on, and the engine starts them in dependency order, releasing the next stage only when a container is actually ready — not merely "running". It adds per-container CPU, RAM and bandwidth limits (with built-in proof), a compact actions column, and clean, themeable badges that show live state at a glance. The Plugins and VM tabs pick up the matching styling. A small Go daemon on a local unix socket does the work — nothing leaves your server.

Highlights

  • Start plan — dependency-ordered, readiness-gated start stages, so an app waits for its database to be genuinely ready, not merely "running"
  • Readiness probes — running (with grace) / TCP port / HTTP / log-line match / exec-in-container, with per-node failure policies (abort dependents, continue, or degrade)
  • Watchdog & schedules — auto-restart on crash or unhealthy (rate-capped per hour) and start/stop/restart at fixed times, per weekday
  • Idle auto-stop (ContainerNursery-inspired) — stop a container after it stays idle on both CPU and network; busy or host-network containers are skipped
  • Live resource limits — CPU limit, CPU pinning (topology-aware, P/E-core detection on Intel hybrid CPUs) and RAM limit via docker update, no restart
  • Bandwidth limits — per-container upload (tbf shaper) and download (pure netfilter policing, never a tc ingress qdisc), applied inside the container's own network namespace
  • Built-in proof — the editors read the LIVE rule back from the container and show either the applied state or the exact failure; a silent no-op is impossible
  • Actions column & clean badges — state, network, IP (click to copy), ports, volumes and update status, with both a list view and a card (grid) view
  • Theming — one accent colour for everything or rainbow mode, a configurable badge shape, and a master theming-off switch that keeps only the orchestration
  • Bereiche (Areas) — turn each surface on or off independently: Docker, Plugins and VM tabs, plus (opt-in) the main menu bar and the Settings-page icons
  • Local-only — a small Go daemon on a local unix socket; nothing leaves the server. Installed as an Unraid plugin (.plg)

Requirements / Install

CannonadeCommand is an Unraid plugin, installed by URL. In Unraid go to Plugins → Install Plugin and paste:

https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/cannonadecommand/main/plugin/cannonadecommand.plg

The plugin installs the UI pages and starts the engine (a single Go daemon on a local unix socket). The Install on Unraid button above is for once it lands in Community Applications.

  • Unraid 6.10+
  • Docker (and, optionally, the VM manager for the VM tab)
  • No reverse proxy needed — the UI is served by Unraid and the engine listens on a local unix socket

Posting a bug report

Please post:

  • Unraid version (Settings → System Information)
  • Plugin version
  • The running UI + engine versions (the table-header gear on the Docker tab shows both)
  • The specific tab and action you were on (e.g. Docker → bandwidth gear → save)
  • Browser + OS you're using
  • The daemon log (Settings → Utilities → CannonadeCommand diagnostics card, or /var/log for the plugin)

GitHub issues with the same info are also welcome: github.com/junkerderprovinz/cannonadecommand/issues

Credits

Built in Go for Unraid's native Docker, Plugins and VM tabs. The idle auto-stop is inspired by ContainerNursery. MIT licensed. If CannonadeCommand tightens up your Unraid, you can buy me a coffee.

Self-hosted, MIT-licensed — you run it, you own your server and the responsibility.
